spec_match.rationale: Uses getOrInsertComputed exactly as the TC39 motivating example prescribes — the four-line has/get/set dance collapsed to one call, factory only invoked on miss.

interactive.rationale: Real inputs (fib(n) value, run, reset) drive a live side-by-side memoised computation with hit/miss counters, last-call timings, and a scrolling log. Concrete, hands-on.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb: 'canonical TC39 motivation: collapse has + get + compute + set into one call. Side-by-side hit/miss counters on a memoized Fibonacci.' — page delivers exactly that.

edge_cases.rationale: Input range 0..40 is enforced but the page never demonstrates the laziness contract: that the factory is genuinely skipped on a hit. A timing differential is shown indirectly via 'last call ms', but there is no explicit factory-invocation counter to prove the lazy semantics.

references.rationale: Sees-also links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5201653661827072 and the TC39 proposal.

design_system.rationale: CSS variables throughout; the .panel.win green panel uses --accent-emerald with default --text-black which still meets AA (4.6:1).

openQuestions.title: Add a factory-invocations counter
openQuestions.detail: Hits and misses are tracked, but the page never shows that getOrInsertComputed's factory is invoked exactly once per missed key. Add a 'factory calls' counter that increments inside the computed callback; on a hit it should not move. This is the headline guarantee the page is built to teach.

openQuestions.title: Contrast with the `map.get(k) ?? map.set(k, compute()).get(k)` foot-gun
openQuestions.detail: The 'why this angle' copy mentions the eager-evaluation hack, but doesn't show it side-by-side. A third panel that uses the nullish-coalescing trick and counts factory calls would visually demonstrate the eager-evaluation cost the proposal eliminates.

openQuestions.title: Show factory error propagation
openQuestions.detail: If `compute` throws inside getOrInsertComputed the map stays unchanged and the error propagates. A 'force-throw on next miss' toggle would teach this and is currently invisible.

summary: Clean canonical-example demo with live counters. To make the lazy-evaluation guarantee tangible, surface a factory-invocations counter and contrast against the `?? .set().get()` foot-gun pattern.
